message:
test(controller): tighten #109 guard to per-line check; declutter example text

Follow-up to c6e15d97. Two refinements:

1. Remove the "(positional arg, no --msg flag)" aside from the prompt
   example for `ntm send ... --pane N "message"`. The longhand was
   helpful as a defensive footnote but made the example itself contain
   the literal substring `--msg`, which is the exact anti-pattern we
   want the test to forbid elsewhere.

2. Strengthen both controller tests so they no longer scan the whole
   prompt as one blob. Instead they walk the prompt line-by-line and
   fail if any single line mixes the `ntm send` subcommand with a
   `--msg` flag. This catches the real regression shape (a copy-paste
   example that pairs the subcommand with the unsupported flag) while
   still allowing the legitimate `ntm --robot-send=... --msg=...` line
   on its own. A substring check on the entire prompt would either
   miss the regression or forbid the valid `--robot-send` example.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -63,7 +63,7 @@ State inspection (read-only, safe to call in a loop; pass SESSION as the flag va
 - ntm --robot-mail-check --mail-project={{.Session}}       - Check Agent Mail inbox for pending messages
 
 Actions (mutating; use deliberately):
-- ntm send {{.Session}} --pane N "message"                 - Send message to a single pane (positional arg, no --msg flag)
+- ntm send {{.Session}} --pane N "message"                 - Send message to a single pane (the message is a positional argument)
 - ntm send {{.Session}} --panes=1,2 "message"              - Send to multiple panes
 - ntm --robot-send={{.Session}} --panes=N --msg="..."      - Robot equivalent with structured JSON response
 - ntm --robot-interrupt={{.Session}} --panes=N             - Interrupt a pane without killing it
